channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Fake Call History Apps Stole Payments From Users After 7.3 Million Play Store Downloads
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered fraudulent apps on the official Google Play Store for Android that falsely claimed to offer access to call histories for any phone number, only to trick users...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: One Click, Total Shutdown: The "Patient Zero" Webinar on Killing Stealth Breaches
item.description: The hardest part of cybersecurity isn't the technology, it’s the people. Every major breach you’ve read about lately usually starts the same way: one employee, one clever email, and one "Patient Zero"...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: One size does not fit all — sometimes, victims probably should pay ransom
item.description: DataBreaches posted the following opinion piece on LinkedIn this morning in my Dissent Doe, PhD account: Last night, Canvas was restored, and the Instructure leak site listing was removed from the thr...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: Quasar Linux RAT Steals Developer Credentials for Software Supply Chain Compromise
item.description: A previously undocumented Linux implant codenamed Quasar Linux RAT (QLNX) is targeting developers' systems to establish a silent foothold as well as facilitate a broad range of post-compromise functio...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: One Missed Threat Per Week: What 25M Alerts Reveal About Low-Severity Risk
item.description: The dark secret of enterprise security operations is that defenders have quietly institutionalized the practice of not looking. This is not just anecdotal, but rather backed by a recent report investi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Linux PamDOORa Backdoor Uses PAM Modules to Steal SSH Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux backdoor named PamDOORa that's being advertised on the Rehub Russian cybercrime forum for $1,600 by a threat actor called "darkworm." Th...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Linux Kernel Dirty Frag LPE Exploit Enables Root Access Across Major Distributions
item.description: Details have emerged about a new, unpatched local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability impacting the Linux kernel. Dubbed Dirty Frag, it has been described as a successor to Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
item.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, af...
item.dc:creator: Krebs On Security

item.title: Developing: ShinyHunters Hacks Instructure Again; Canvas Down (1)
item.description: When Instructure did not contact ShinyHunters to negotiate any payment after ShinyHunters attacked them for a second time in April, the threat actors threatened to leak every school’s data, and poste...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: After Replacing TeamPCP Malware, 'PCPJack' Steals Cloud Secrets
item.description: PCPJack makes innovative use of parquet files for stealthy, pre-validated target discovery as it canvasses multiple cloud environments....

item.title: Has CISA Finally Found Its New Leader in Tom Parker?

item.title: Ivanti EPMM CVE-2026-6973 RCE Under Active Exploitation Grants Admin-Level Access
item.description: Ivanti is warning that a new security flaw impacting Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) has been explored in limited attacks in the wild. The high-severity vulnerability, CVE-2026-6973 (CVSS score: 7.2), ...
